title: dodecatheon meadia subsp. eumeadia
intro: Dodecatheon meadia subsp. eumeadia, also known as the western pony, is a perennial wildflower belonging to the primrose family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Dodecatheon meadia subsp. eumeadia include soothing skin irritations and reducing the severity of cold symptoms. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical or internal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
